_comment: Cook Mode step overlays, keyed by recipe id. Recipes themselves stay read-only (src/data/recipes.json); these add the discrete-step / doneness / technique structure Coach Mode needs. Seed slice = one recipe authored end-to-end (PRD §13-M0).

steps.text: Squeeze lime over the chicken and rub it in well, then drain off the excess juice.
Combine the tomato, onion, garlic, pepper, thyme, allspice and soy sauce in a large bowl with the chicken. Cover and marinate in the fridge at least one hour.
steps.sensory_cue: Chicken fully coated and submerged; refrigerated, not left on the counter.

steps.text: Heat the oil in a Dutch pot or large saucepan over high heat. Shake the seasonings off each piece of chicken as you lift it from the marinade — reserve the marinade for the sauce.
steps.sensory_cue: Oil shimmers and a drop of water sizzles instantly.

steps.text: Lightly brown the chicken a few pieces at a time in the hot oil, then set browned pieces aside on a plate.
steps.sensory_cue: Each side is deep golden-brown and releases easily from the pan. This is for flavor — the chicken is NOT done yet.

steps.text: Drain off excess oil and return all the chicken to the pan. Pour the reserved marinade over it, add the carrots, and cook over medium heat for 10 minutes, stirring.
Mix the cornstarch into the coconut milk until smooth, stir it into the stew, then turn the heat to low and cook another 20 minutes until the sauce coats a spoon and the chicken is cooked through.
steps.sensory_cue: Sauce is glossy and coats the back of a spoon; chicken is white throughout with clear juices.
